name: Service First
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving South Dakota since 1934, offering mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and deposit accounts with no application fees.
description_long:
Service First Federal Credit Union has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution since 1934, rooted in the cooperative principle of "People Helping People." The organization is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, with a routing number (291479903) and NMLS ID (313985) confirming its regulatory status. The credit union emphasizes community involvement and member-focused service as core values.

Service First offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including mortgage loans for home purchases, auto loans with no application or origination fees, personal loans, share certificates (savings accounts), and checking/savings accounts for both personal and business members. They provide online banking access, loan calculators for financial planning, and in-person consultation at physical branch locations. Auto loan rates start as low as 4.70% APR, personal loans from 9.30% APR, and share certificates yielding up to 3.00% APY.

The credit union distinguishes itself primarily through its no-fee loan origination policy on auto loans and its emphasis on personalized service. They position themselves as community-focused, evidenced by documented charitable donations to local nonprofits like Calm Waters and The Banquet in Sioux Falls. The institution operates physical branch locations and maintains a membership model that emphasizes profit-sharing with members rather than external shareholders.

Service First is a straightforward credit union with standard offerings but limited information publicly available about membership requirements, asset size, or competitive rate positioning relative to national alternatives. The website provides rate ranges and calculators but lacks detailed product specifications, fee schedules, or member testimonials. For consumers in South Dakota with access to branches, this appears to be a legitimate community-based option, though geographic service area limitations may restrict eligibility.
services: Mortgage loans for home purchase with competitive rates
Auto loans with no application or origination fees
Online banking with member login portal
Loan payment calculator and financial planning tools
In-branch loan consultation and account services
pros: No application or origination fees on auto loans, reducing total borrowing costs
Federally chartered and NCUA-insured, providing member deposit insurance up to $250,000
Competitive rate offerings with auto loans starting at 4.70% APR and personal loans from 9.30% APR
Share certificates (CDs) offering up to 3.00% APY for savers
In-person branch locations available for face-to-face consultation with loan officers
Member-owned structure means profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders
Established 90+ year history (founded 1934) demonstrates organizational stability
cons: Geographic limitation likely restricts membership to South Dakota residents or specific communities
Website does not clearly outline membership eligibility requirements or qualification criteria
Limited online information about full fee schedule, minimum deposit requirements, or product details
No public data on competitive positioning or member satisfaction/reviews
Mortgage and personal loan application processes require branch visits or online submission with unclear timelines
best_for: South Dakota residents seeking mortgages or auto loans with no origination fees
Community-minded consumers who prioritize member-owned institutions and local reinvestment
Borrowers wanting in-person consultation and personalized service from local loan officers
Savers looking for competitive CD/share certificate rates from a federally insured institution
diagnosis: Service First is best suited for South Dakota residents who value community-based banking and want to avoid loan origination fees on auto purchases or mortgages. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears geographically restricted, and the institution provides limited online transparency about specific terms, rates, and full product details compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
